facts: 571.54M reviews from 54.51M users across 48.19M items in 33 categories (May 1996 - Sept 2023).
hypotheses: Category-cluster, rather than raw category, is the appropriate environment unit because several categories are near-duplicates.
Temporal shift within a category dominates cross-category shift.
intuitions: CDR leaderboards 2024-25 and McAuley group post-2023 releases likely cover this comparison.
unknowns: Whether user-as-bridge across categories is genuine cross-domain signal or merely correlation cannot be determined from the data.
Whether the 33-category commercial taxonomy matches a semantic taxonomy is undecidable from the data.
